{\n171\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status %d, got %d\", code, ev.response.Code)\n172\t\t\t\t\treturn nil\n173\t\t\t\t}\n174\t\t\t\treturn nil\n175\t\t\t},\n176\t\t})\n177\t\treturn v\n178\t}\n179\t\n180\t// ContentType verifies the Content-Type header.\n181\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) ContentType(contentType string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n182\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n183\t\t\tname: \"ContentType\",\n184\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n185\t\t\t\tactual := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Content-Type\")\n186\t\t\t\tif actual != contentType {\n187\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected Content-Type '%s', got '%s'\", contentType, actual)\n188\t\t\t\t}\n189\t\t\t\treturn nil\n190\t\t\t},\n191\t\t})\n192\t\treturn v\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// HasCode verifies the S3 error code.\n196\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasCode(code string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n197\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n198\t\t\tname: \"HasCode\",\n199\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n200\t\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n201\t\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); err != nil {\n202\t\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n203\t\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n204\t\t\t\t\t}\n205\t\t\t\t}\n206\t\t\t\tif ev.s3Error.Code != code {\n207\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", code, ev.s3Error.Code)\n208\t\t\t\t}\n209\t\t\t\treturn nil\n210\t\t\t},\n211\t\t})\n212\t\treturn v\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\t// HasMessage verifies the error message contains expected text.\n216\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasMessage(substr string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n217\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n218\t\t\tname: \"HasMessage\",\n219\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n220\t\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n221\t\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); err != nil {\n222\t\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n223\t\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n224\t\t\t\t\t}\n225\t\t\t\t}\n226\t\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(ev.s3Error.Message, substr) {\n227\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", substr, ev.s3Error.Message)\n228\t\t\t\t}\n229\t\t\t\treturn nil\n230\t\t\t},\n231\t\t})\n232\t\treturn v\n233\t}\n234\t\n235\t// MessageMinLength verifies the error message is at least n characters.\n236\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) MessageMinLength(minLen int) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n237\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n238\t\t\tname: \"MessageMinLength\",\n239\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n240\t\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n241\t\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); err != nil {\n242\t\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n243\t\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n244\t\t\t\t\t}\n245\t\t\t\t}\n246\t\t\t\tif len(ev.s3Error.Message) < minLen {\n247\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n248\t\t\t\t\t\tlen(ev.s3Error.Message), minLen, ev.s3Error.Message)\n249\t\t\t\t}\n250\t\t\t\treturn nil\n251\t\t\t},\n252\t\t})\n253\t\treturn v\n254\t}\n255\t\n256\t// ResponseTime verifies the response time is under the threshold.\n257\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) ResponseTime(maxDuration time.Duration) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n258\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n259\t\t\tname: \"ResponseTime\",\n260\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n261\t\t\t\tif v.measuredDuration > maxDuration {\n262\t\t\t\t\tv.t.Errorf(\"Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", v.measuredDuration, maxDuration)\n263\t\t\t\t}\n264\t\t\t\treturn nil\n265\t\t\t},\n266\t\t})\n267\t\treturn v\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// HasXMLDeclaration verifies the response starts with XML declaration.\n271\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasXMLDeclaration() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n272\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n273\t\t\tname: \"HasXMLDeclaration\",\n274\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n275\t\t\t\tbody := v.response.Body.String()\n276\t\t\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n277\t\t\t\t\tv.t.Errorf(\"Expected XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n278\t\t\t\t}\n279\t\t\t\treturn nil\n280\t\t\t},\n281\t\t})\n282\t\treturn v\n283\t}\n284\t\n285\t// BodyNotEmpty verifies the response body is not empty.\n286\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) BodyNotEmpty() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n287\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n288\t\t\tname: \"BodyNotEmpty\",\n289\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n290\t\t\t\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n291\t\t\t\t\tv.t.Error(\"Expected non-empty response body\")\n292\t\t\t\t}\n293\t\t\t\treturn nil\n294\t\t\t},\n295\t\t})\n296\t\treturn v\n297\t}\n298\t\n299\t// MessageContainsAny verifies the message contains at least one of the keywords.\n300\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) MessageContainsAny(keywords ...string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n301\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n302\t\t\tname: \"MessageContainsAny\",\n303\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n304\t\t\t\tif ev.s3Error == nil {\n305\t\t\t\t\tif err := ev.parse(); err != nil {\n306\t\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n307\t\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n308\t\t\t\t\t}\n309\t\t\t\t}\n310\t\t\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(ev.s3Error.Message)\n311\t\t\t\tfound := false\n312\t\t\t\tfor _, keyword := range keywords {\n313\t\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n314\t\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n315\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n316\t\t\t\t\t}\n317\t\t\t\t}\n318\t\t\t\tif !found {\n319\t\t\t\t\tv.t.Errorf(\"Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n320\t\t\t\t\t\tkeywords, ev.s3Error.Message)\n321\t\t\t\t}\n322\t\t\t\treturn nil\n323\t\t\t},\n324\t\t})\n325\t\treturn v\n326\t}\n327\t\n328\t// HasCORSHeaders verifies that CORS headers are present on the error response.\n329\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) HasCORSHeaders() *ErrorResponseValidator {\n330\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n331\t\t\tname: \"HasCORSHeaders\",\n332\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n333\t\t\t\torigin := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n334\t\t\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n335\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n336\t\t\t\t}\n337\t\t\t\tmethods := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n338\t\t\t\tif methods == \"\" {\n339\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n340\t\t\t\t}\n341\t\t\t\theaders := ev.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n342\t\t\t\tif headers == \"\" {\n343\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n344\t\t\t\t}\n345\t\t\t\treturn nil\n346\t\t\t},\n347\t\t})\n348\t\treturn v\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// CORSOrigin verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n352\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSOrigin(origin string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n353\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n354\t\t\tname: \"CORSOrigin\",\n355\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n356\t\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n357\t\t\t\tif actual != origin {\n358\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", origin, actual)\n359\t\t\t\t}\n360\t\t\t\treturn nil\n361\t\t\t},\n362\t\t})\n363\t\treturn v\n364\t}\n365\t\n366\t// CORSMethods verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n367\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSMethods(methods string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n368\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n369\t\t\tname: \"CORSMethods\",\n370\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n371\t\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n372\t\t\t\tif actual != methods {\n373\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", methods, actual)\n374\t\t\t\t}\n375\t\t\t\treturn nil\n376\t\t\t},\n377\t\t})\n378\t\treturn v\n379\t}\n380\t\n381\t// CORSHeaders verifies the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n382\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) CORSHeaders(headers string) *ErrorResponseValidator {\n383\t\tv.expectations = append(v.expectations, expectation{\n384\t\t\tname: \"CORSHeaders\",\n385\t\t\tvalidate: func(ev *ErrorResponseValidator) error {\n386\t\t\t\tactual := v.response.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n387\t\t\t\tif actual != headers {\n388\t\t\t\t\tev.t.Errorf(\"Expected CORS headers '%s', got '%s'\", headers, actual)\n389\t\t\t\t}\n390\t\t\t\treturn nil\n391\t\t\t},\n392\t\t})\n393\t\treturn v\n394\t}\n395\t\n396\t// Assert runs all validations and asserts if any fail.\n397\t// This is the terminal method - call it to execute all validations.\n398\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n399\t\tv.t.Helper()\n400\t\n401\t\t// Parse response once for all validations\n402\t\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n403\t\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n404\t\t\treturn\n405\t\t}\n406\t\n407\t\t// Run all validations\n408\t\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n409\t\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n410\t\t\t\texp.failed = true\n411\t\t\t}\n412\t\t}\n413\t}\n414\t\n415\t// parse parses the S3 error response.\n416\tfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n417\t\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n418\t\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n419\t\t}\n420\t\n421\t\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n422\t\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n423\t\t}\n424\t\n425\t\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n426\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n427\t\t\treturn err\n428\t\t}\n429\t\n430\t\treturn nil\n431\t}\n432\t\n433\t// =============================================================================\n434\t// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n435\t// =============================================================================\n436\t\n437\t// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n438\t// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n439\t//\n440\t// It verifies:\n441\t// - HTTP status code matches expected\n442\t// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n443\t// - Response body is not empty\n444\t// - Error code matches expected\n445\t// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n446\t// - Response starts with XML declaration\n447\tfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n448\t\tt.Helper()\n449\t\n450\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n451\t\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n452\t\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n453\t\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n454\t\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n455\t\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n456\t\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n457\t\t\tAssert()\n458\t}\n459\t\n460\t// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\n461\tfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n462\t\tt.Helper()\n463\t\n464\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n465\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n466\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n467\t\t}\n468\t\n469\t\treturn &s3Err\n470\t}\n471\t\n472\t// =============================================================================\n473\t// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n474\t// =============================================================================\n475\t// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n476\t// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n477\t// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n478\t// =============================================================================\n479\t\n480\t// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n481\t//\n482\t// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n483\t// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n484\t// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n485\t//\n486\t// Example:\n487\t//   ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\n488\tfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n489\t\tt.Helper()\n490\t\n491\t\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n492\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n493\t\t}\n494\t}\n495\t\n496\t// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n497\t//\n498\t// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n499\t// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n500\t// ValidateContentType is defined in content_type_validation.go with enhanced functionality.\n501\t\n502\t// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n503\t//\n504\t// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n505\t// - A non-empty response body\n506\t// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n507\t// - A non-empty error code\n508\t// - A non-empty error message\n509\t//\n510\t// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n511\t//\n512\t// Example:\n513\t//   ValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\n514\tfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n515\t\tt.Helper()\n516\t\n517\t\t// Check response body is not empty\n518\t\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n519\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n520\t\t}\n521\t\n522\t\t// Parse the error response\n523\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n524\t\n525\t\t// Validate error code is present\n526\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n527\t\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n528\t\t}\n529\t\n530\t\t// Validate error message is present\n531\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n532\t\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n533\t\t}\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n537\t//\n538\t// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n539\t// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n540\t//\n541\t// Example:\n542\t//   ValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\n543\tfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n544\t\tt.Helper()\n545\t\n546\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n547\t\n548\t\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n549\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n550\t\t}\n551\t}\n552\t\n553\t// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n554\t//\n555\t// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n556\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n557\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n558\t// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n559\t//\n560\t// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n561\t//\n562\t// Example:\n563\t//   ValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\n564\tfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n565\t\tt.Helper()\n566\t\n567\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n568\t\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n569\t\tif origin == \"\" {\n570\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n571\t\t}\n572\t\n573\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n574\t\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n575\t\tif methods == \"\" {\n576\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n577\t\t}\n578\t\n579\t\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n580\t\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n581\t\tif headers == \"\" {\n582\t\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n583\t\t}\n584\t}\n585\t\n586\t// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n587\t//\n588\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n589\t// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n590\t//\n591\t// Example:\n592\t//   ValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\n593\tfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n594\t\tt.Helper()\n595\t\n596\t\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n597\t\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n598\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n599\t\t}\n600\t}\n601\t\n602\t// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n603\t//\n604\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n605\t// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n606\t//\n607\t// Example:\n608\t//   ValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\n609\tfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n610\t\tt.Helper()\n611\t\n612\t\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n613\t\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n614\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n615\t\t}\n616\t}\n617\t\n618\t// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n619\t//\n620\t// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n621\t// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n622\t//\n623\t// Example:\n624\t//   ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\n625\tfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n626\t\tt.Helper()\n627\t\n628\t\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n629\t\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n630\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n631\t\t}\n632\t}\n633\t\n634\t// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n635\t//\n636\t// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n637\t// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n638\t//\n639\t// Example:\n640\t//   ValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\n641\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n642\t\tt.Helper()\n643\t\n644\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n645\t\n646\t\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n647\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n648\t\t}\n649\t}\n650\t\n651\t// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n652\t//\n653\t// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n654\t// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n655\t//\n656\t// Example:\n657\t//   ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\n658\tfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n659\t\tt.Helper()\n660\t\n661\t\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n662\t\n663\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n664\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n665\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n666\t\t}\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n670\t//\n671\t// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n672\t// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n673\t//\n674\t// Example:\n675\t//   ValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\n676\tfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n677\t\tt.Helper()\n678\t\n679\t\tbody := w.Body.String()\n680\t\n681\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n682\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n683\t\t}\n684\t}\n685\t\n686\t// =============================================================================\n687\t// ERROR RESPONSE STRUCTURE VALIDATION\n688\t// =============================================================================\n689\t// These helpers provide comprehensive error response structure validation\n690\t// with support for optional field validation and detailed error reporting.\n691\t// =============================================================================\n692\t\n693\t// ErrorStructureValidationOptions configures error structure validation.\n694\ttype ErrorStructureValidationOptions struct {\n695\t\t// RequireCode ensures the error code field is present and non-empty\n696\t\tRequireCode bool\n697\t\t// RequireMessage ensures the error message field is present and non-empty\n698\t\tRequireMessage bool\n699\t\t// ExpectedCode checks that the error code matches the expected value\n700\t\tExpectedCode string\n701\t\t// MinMessageLength ensures the error message meets minimum length\n702\t\tMinMessageLength int\n703\t\t// MessageContains checks that the error message contains specific text\n704\t\tMessageContains string\n705\t\t// CustomFields validates additional custom fields in the error response\n706\t\tCustomFields map[string]string\n707\t}\n708\t\n709\t// DefaultValidationOptions returns default validation options.\n710\tfunc DefaultValidationOptions() ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n711\t\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n712\t\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n713\t\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n714\t\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n715\t\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n716\t\t}\n717\t}\n718\t\n719\t// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates the structure of an error response.\n720\t//\n721\t// This helper function performs comprehensive validation of error response structure:\n722\t// - Validates response body is not empty\n723\t// - Validates error field exists (Code field)\n724\t// - Validates error message is present and non-empty\n725\t// - Supports optional field validation via options\n726\t//\n727\t// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n728\t// Note: This function does not call t.Error() to allow testing of invalid cases.\n729\t// Use AssertValidErrorResponseStructure() for assertion behavior.\n730\t//\n731\t// Parameters:\n732\t//   t: Testing instance for context\n733\t//   body: Response body as byte array\n734\t//   options: Validation options (use DefaultValidationOptions() for defaults)\n735\t//\n736\t// Example:\n737\t//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n738\t//   opts.RequireCode = true\n739\t//   opts.RequireMessage = true\n740\t//   opts.MinMessageLength = 15\n741\t//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody, opts)\n742\tfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n743\t\tt.Helper()\n744\t\n745\t\t// Check response body is not empty\n746\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n747\t\t\treturn false\n748\t\t}\n749\t\n750\t\t// Parse the error response\n751\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n752\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n753\t\t\treturn false\n754\t\t}\n755\t\n756\t\t// Track validation results\n757\t\tallValid := true\n758\t\n759\t\t// Validate error code field exists if required\n760\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n761\t\t\tallValid = false\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\t// Validate error message field exists if required\n765\t\tif options.RequireMessage {\n766\t\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n767\t\t\tif trimmedMsg == \"\" {\n768\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n769\t\t\t}\n770\t\t}\n771\t\n772\t\t// Validate expected code if specified\n773\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n774\t\t\tallValid = false\n775\t\t}\n776\t\n777\t\t// Validate minimum message length if specified\n778\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n779\t\t\t// Use trimmed message for length validation\n780\t\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n781\t\t\tif len(trimmedMsg) < options.MinMessageLength {\n782\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n783\t\t\t}\n784\t\t}\n785\t\n786\t\t// Validate message contains expected text if specified (case-insensitive)\n787\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" {\n788\t\t\tmessageLower := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n789\t\t\tkeywordLower := strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)\n790\t\t\tif !strings.Contains(messageLower, keywordLower) {\n791\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n792\t\t\t}\n793\t\t}\n794\t\n795\t\t// Validate custom fields if specified\n796\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n797\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n798\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n799\t\t\t\tallValid = false\n800\t\t\t}\n801\t\t}\n802\t\n803\t\treturn allValid\n804\t}\n805\t\n806\t// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with defaults.\n807\t//\n808\t// This is a simplified version that uses default validation options.\n809\t// It validates:\n810\t// - Response body is not empty\n811\t// - Error code field exists and is non-empty\n812\t// - Error message field exists and is non-empty (min 10 chars)\n813\t//\n814\t// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n815\t//\n816\t// Example:\n817\t//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, responseBody)\n818\tfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n819\t\tt.Helper()\n820\t\treturn ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, DefaultValidationOptions())\n821\t}\n822\t\n823\t// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure validates error structure and asserts if invalid.\n824\t//\n825\t// This helper validates error response structure and fails the test if validation fails.\n826\t// Use this when you want test execution to stop on validation failure.\n827\t// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n828\t//\n829\t// Example:\n830\t//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\n831\tfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte) {\n832\t\tt.Helper()\n833\t\n834\t\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body) {\n835\t\t\t// Provide detailed error message\n836\t\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n837\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n838\t\t\t}\n839\t\n840\t\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n841\t\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n842\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n843\t\t\t}\n844\t\n845\t\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n846\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n847\t\t\t}\n848\t\n849\t\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n850\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n851\t\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < 10 {\n854\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least 10): %s\",\n855\t\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), s3Err.Message)\n856\t\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n859\t\t}\n860\t}\n861\t\n862\t// AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions validates with custom options and asserts.\n863\t//\n864\t// This helper validates error response structure with custom options and fails the test\n865\t// if validation fails. Use this when you need specific validation requirements.\n866\t// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n867\t//\n868\t// Example:\n869\t//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n870\t//   opts.MinMessageLength = 20\n871\t//   opts.MessageContains = \"authentication\"\n872\t//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, responseBody, opts)\n873\tfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n874\t\tt.Helper()\n875\t\n876\t\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options) {\n877\t\t\t// Provide detailed error message based on options\n878\t\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n879\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n880\t\t\t}\n881\t\n882\t\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n883\t\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n884\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n885\t\t\t}\n886\t\n887\t\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n888\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n889\t\t\t}\n890\t\n891\t\t\tif options.RequireMessage && s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n892\t\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n893\t\t\t}\n894\t\n895\t\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n896\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected code '%s', got '%s'\",\n897\t\t\t\t\toptions.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n898\t\t\t}\n899\t\n900\t\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(s3Err.Message) < options.MinMessageLength {\n901\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n902\t\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), options.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n903\t\t\t}\n904\t\n905\t\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, options.MessageContains) {\n906\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n907\t\t\t\t\toptions.MessageContains, s3Err.Message)\n908\t\t\t}\n909\t\n910\t\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n911\t\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n912\t\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n913\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected field '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\",\n914\t\t\t\t\t\tfield, expectedValue, actualValue)\n915\t\t\t\t}\n916\t\t\t}\n917\t\n918\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n919\t\t}\n920\t}\n921\t\n922\t// getXMLField extracts a field value from XML response body.\n923\t// This is a helper for validating custom fields in error responses.\n924\tfunc getXMLField(body []byte, field string) string {\n925\t\t// Simple XML field extraction using string search\n926\t\t// This is basic but works for common S3 error response structures\n927\t\topenTag := \"<\" + field + \">\"\n928\t\tcloseTag := \"</\" + field + \">\"\n929\t\n930\t\tbodyStr := string(body)\n931\t\topenIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr, openTag)\n932\t\tif openIdx == -1 {\n933\t\t\treturn \"\"\n934\t\t}\n935\t\n936\t\tcloseIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr[openIdx:], closeTag)\n937\t\tif closeIdx == -1 {\n938\t\t\treturn \"\"\n939\t\t}\n940\t\n941\t\treturn strings.TrimSpace(bodyStr[openIdx+len(openTag):openIdx+closeIdx])\n942\t}\n943\t\n944\t// =============================================================================\n945\t// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n946\t// =============================================================================\n947\t\n948\t// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n949\t//\n950\t// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n951\t// It supports:\n952\t// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n953\t// - Optional request body\n954\t// - Custom headers\n955\t// - Test server URL format\n956\tfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n957\t\tt.Helper()\n958\t\n959\t\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n960\t\n961\t\t// Set default headers for test requests\n962\t\tif headers == nil {\n963\t\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n964\t\t}\n965\t\n966\t\t// Set custom headers\n967\t\tfor key, value := range headers {\n968\t\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n969\t\t}\n970\t\n971\t\treturn req\n972\t}\n973\t\n974\t// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n975\t//\n976\t// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n977\t// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\n978\tfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n979\t\tt.Helper()\n980\t\n981\t\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n982\t\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n983\t}\n984\t\n985\t\n986\t// =============================================================================\n987\t// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n988\t// =============================================================================\n989\t\n990\t// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\n991\ttype ErrorScenario struct {\n992\t\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n993\t\tName string\n994\t\n995\t\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n996\t\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n997\t\n998\t\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n999\t\tExpectedCode string\n1000\t\n1001\t\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n1002\t\tExpectedStatus int\n1003\t\n1004\t\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n1005\t\tExpectedKeywords []string\n1006\t\n1007\t\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n1008\t\tMinMessageLength int\n1009\t\n1010\t\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n1011\t\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n1012\t\n1013\t\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n1014\t\tDescription string\n1015\t}\n1016\t\n1017\t// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\n1018\ttype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n1019\t\tScenarioName string\n1020\t\tStatusCode   int\n1021\t\tErrorCode    string\n1022\t\tMessage      string\n1023\t\tDuration     time.Duration\n1024\t\tPassed       bool\n1025\t}\n1026\t\n1027\t// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\n1028\tfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n1029\t\tt.Helper()\n1030\t\n1031\t\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n1032\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1033\t\n1034\t\t// Measure response time\n1035\t\tstart := time.Now()\n1036\t\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n1037\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n1038\t\n1039\t\t// Parse response\n1040\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n1041\t\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n1042\t\n1043\t\t// Build result\n1044\t\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n1045\t\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n1046\t\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n1047\t\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n1048\t\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n1049\t\t\tDuration:     duration,\n1050\t\t}\n1051\t\n1052\t\t// Validate result\n1053\t\tresult.Passed = true\n1054\t\n1055\t\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n1056\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n1057\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1058\t\t}\n1059\t\n1060\t\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n1061\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n1062\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1063\t\t}\n1064\t\n1065\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < scenario.MinMessageLength {\n1066\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n1067\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, len(s3Err.Message), scenario.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n1068\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1069\t\t}\n1070\t\n1071\t\t// Check keywords if specified\n1072\t\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n1073\t\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n1074\t\t\tfound := false\n1075\t\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n1076\t\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n1077\t\t\t\t\tfound = true\n1078\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1079\t\t\t\t}\n1080\t\t\t}\n1081\t\t\tif !found {\n1082\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n1083\t\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedKeywords, s3Err.Message)\n1084\t\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1085\t\t\t}\n1086\t\t}\n1087\t\n1088\t\t// Check response time if specified\n1089\t\tif scenario.MaxResponseTime > 0 && duration > scenario.MaxResponseTime {\n1090\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", scenario.Name, duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n1091\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n1092\t\t}\n1093\t\n1094\t\treturn result\n1095\t}\n1096\t\n1097\t// =============================================================================\n1098\t// BASE TEST STRUCTURES\n1099\t// =============================================================================\n1100\t\n1101\t// ErrorTestSuite provides a base structure for error response test suites.\n1102\t//\n1103\t// This structure helps organize related error tests and provides\n1104\t// consistent reporting and validation across test types.\n1105\ttype ErrorTestSuite struct {\n1106\t\tt          *testing.T\n1107\t\tname       string\n1108\t\tfixture    *TestServerFixture\n1109\t\tscenarios  []ErrorScenario\n1110\t\tresults    []*ErrorScenarioResult\n1111\t}\n1112\t\n1113\t// NewErrorTestSuite creates a new error test suite.\n1114\tfunc NewErrorTestSuite(t *testing.T, name string) *ErrorTestSuite {\n1115\t\tt.Helper()\n1116\t\n1117\t\treturn &ErrorTestSuite{\n1118\t\t\tt:       t,\n1119\t\t\tname:    name,\n1120\t\t\tfixture: NewTestServer(t),\n1121\t\t\tresults: make([]*ErrorScenarioResult, 0),\n1122\t\t}\n1123\t}\n1124\t\n1125\t// AddScenario adds a test scenario to the suite.\n1126\tfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) AddScenario(scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorTestSuite {\n1127\t\tsuite.scenarios = append(suite.scenarios, scenario)\n1128\t\treturn suite\n1129\t}\n1130\t\n1131\t// Run executes all scenarios in the suite.\n1132\tfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) Run() {\n1133\t\tsuite.t.Helper()\n1134\t\n1135\t\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n1136\t\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n1137\t\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n1138\t\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n1139\t\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n1140\t\t\t\t})\n1141\t\t\t}\n1142\t\t})\n1143\t}\n1144\t\n1145\t// RunSequentially executes all scenarios sequentially (not in parallel).\n1146\t// Use this when scenarios have dependencies or share state.\n1147\tfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) RunSequentially() {\n1148\t\tsuite.t.Helper()\n1149\t\n1150\t\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n1151\t\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n1152\t\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n1153\t\t\t\t\tt.Parallel() // Disable parallelism\n1154\t\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n1155\t\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n1156\t\t\t\t})\n1157\t\t\t}\n1158\t\t})\n1159\t}\n1160\t\n1161\t// ReportStatistics logs performance statistics for the suite.\n1162\tfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) ReportStatistics() {\n1163\t\tsuite.t.Helper()\n1164\t\n1165\t\tif len(suite.results) == 0 {\n1166\t\t\tsuite.t.Log(\"No results to report\")\n1167\t\t\treturn\n1168\t\t}\n1169\t\n1170\t\tvar total, max, min time.Duration\n1171\t\tmin = 1 * time.Second // Initialize to high value\n1172\t\tpassed := 0\n1173\t\tfailed := 0\n1174\t\n1175\t\tfor _, result := range suite.results {\n1176\t\t\ttotal += result.Duration\n1177\t\t\tif result.Duration > max {\n1178\t\t\t\tmax = result.Duration\n1179\t\t\t}\n1180\t\t\tif result.Duration < min {\n1181\t\t\t\tmin = result.Duration\n1182\t\t\t}\n1183\t\t\tif result.Passed {\n1184\t\t\t\tpassed++\n1185\t\t\t} else {\n1186\t\t\t\tfailed++\n1187\t\t\t}\n1188\t\t}\n1189\t\n1190\t\tavg := total / time.Duration(len(suite.results))\n1191\t\n1192\t\tsuite.t.Logf(\"Error Test Suite: %s\", suite.name)\n1193\t\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Total scenarios: %d\", len(suite.results))\n1194\t\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Passed: %d, Failed: %d\", passed, failed)\n1195\t\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Average response time: %v\", avg)\n1196\t\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Min response time: %v\", min)\n1197\t\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Max response time: %v\", max)\n1198\t}\n1199\t\n1200\t// =============================================================================\n1201\t// UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n1202\t// =============================================================================\n1203\t\n1204\t// min returns the minimum of two integers.\n1205\tfunc min(a, b int) int {\n1206\t\tif a < b {\n1207\t\t\treturn a\n1208\t\t}\n1209\t\treturn b\n1210\t}\n1211\t\n1212\t// MeasureRequestTime measures the time to execute a request.\n1213\t// Returns the duration and response recorder.\n1214\tfunc MeasureRequestTime(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) (time.Duration, *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n1215\t\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1216\t\tstart := time.Now()\n1217\t\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n1218\t\tduration := time.Since(start)\n1219\t\treturn duration, w\n1220\t}\n1221\t\n1222\t// =============================================================================\n1223\t// DOCUMENTATION AND EXAMPLES\n1224\t// =============================================================================\n1225\t\n1226\t/*\n1227\tExample Usage:\n1228\t\n1229\tBasic error response validation:\n1230\t\n1231\t    func TestMyError(t *testing.T) {\n1232\t        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n1233\t\n1234\t        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n1235\t        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1236\t        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n1237\t\n1238\t        VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\", 404)\n1239\t    }\n1240\t\n1241\tAdvanced validation with fluent API:\n1242\t\n1243\t    func TestMyErrorAdvanced(t *testing.T) {\n1244\t        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n1245\t\n1246\t        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"POST\", \"/test-bucket/test-key\", nil, nil)\n1247\t        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n1248\t        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n1249\t\n1250\t        duration, _ := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n1251\t\n1252\t        VerifyErrorResponseWithTiming(t, w, duration).\n1253\t            HTTPStatusCode(400).\n1254\t            ContentType(\"application/xml\").\n1255\t            HasCode(\"InvalidRequest\").\n1256\t            MessageContainsAny(\"post\", \"unsupported\", \"operation\").\n1257\t            ResponseTime(100 * time.Millisecond).\n1258\t            Assert()\n1259\t    }\n1260\t\n1261\tCORS header validation:\n1262\t\n1263\t    func TestErrorCORSHeaders(t *testing.T) {\n1264\t        fixture := (v *ErrorResponseValidator) Assert() {\n\tv.t.Helper()\n\n\t// Parse response once for all validations\n\tif err := v.parse(); err != nil {\n\t\tv.t.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\t// Run all validations\n\tfor _, exp := range v.expectations {\n\t\tif err := exp.validate(v); err != nil {\n\t\t\texp.failed = true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n\n// parse parses the S3 error response.\nfunc (v *ErrorResponseValidator) parse() error {\n\tif v.s3Error != nil {\n\t\treturn nil // Already parsed\n\t}\n\n\tif v.response.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\treturn nil // Nothing to parse\n\t}\n\n\tv.s3Error = &S3Error{}\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(v.response.Body.Bytes(), v.s3Error); err != nil {\n\t\treturn err\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SIMPLE VALIDATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// VerifyStandardErrorResponse performs standard error response validation.\n// This is a convenience function for common validation patterns.\n//\n// It verifies:\n// - HTTP status code matches expected\n// - Content-Type is \"application/xml\"\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code matches expected\n// - Error message is at least 15 characters\n// - Response starts with XML declaration\nfunc VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string, expectedStatus int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n\t\tHTTPStatusCode(expectedStatus).\n\t\tContentType(\"application/xml\").\n\t\tBodyNotEmpty().\n\t\tHasCode(expectedCode).\n\t\tMessageMinLength(15).\n\t\tHasXMLDeclaration().\n\t\tAssert()\n}\n\n// ParseErrorResponse parses an S3 error response from the response body.\nfunc ParseErrorResponse(t *testing.T, body []byte) *S3Error {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to parse error response: %v\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn &s3Err\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CORE VALIDATION HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n// These are standalone helper functions for validating HTTP error responses.\n// They provide a simpler alternative to the fluent ErrorResponseValidator API.\n// Each helper performs a single validation and can be used independently.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ValidateHTTPStatusCode validates the HTTP status code of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected HTTP status code.\n// It's useful for verifying that error responses return the correct status codes\n// (e.g., 404 for NotFound, 403 for Forbidden, 400 for BadRequest).\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t, w, 404)\nfunc ValidateHTTPStatusCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif w.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected HTTP status code %d, got %d\", expectedCode, w.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateContentType validates the Content-Type header of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the response has the expected Content-Type header.\n// For S3-compatible error responses, this should typically be \"application/xml\".\n// ValidateContentType is defined in content_type_validation.go with enhanced functionality.\n\n// ValidateErrorStructure validates that an error response has proper structure.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains:\n// - A non-empty response body\n// - A valid S3 Error XML structure with Code and Message fields\n// - A non-empty error code\n// - A non-empty error message\n//\n// This ensures the error response is well-formed and contains the required fields.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorStructure(t, w)\nfunc ValidateErrorStructure(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif w.Body.Len() == 0 {\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response body is empty\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\t// Validate error code is present\n\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Code field\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message is present\n\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Error response missing Message field\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorCode validates the S3 error code in an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response contains the expected S3 error code.\n// Common S3 error codes include: NoSuchKey, AccessDenied, InvalidRequest, etc.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorCode(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\")\nfunc ValidateErrorCode(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedCode string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != expectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", expectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSHeaders validates that CORS headers are present on an error response.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response includes the required CORS headers:\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n// - Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n//\n// This ensures that error responses properly support cross-origin requests.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSHeaders(t, w)\nfunc ValidateCORSHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Origin\n\torigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif origin == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Methods\n\tmethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif methods == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods' to be set\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Check Access-Control-Allow-Headers\n\theaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif headers == \"\" {\n\t\tt.Error(\"Expected CORS header 'Access-Control-Allow-Headers' to be set\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSOrigin validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS origin.\n// Common values are \"*\" (allow all origins) or a specific origin.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSOrigin(t, w, \"*\")\nfunc ValidateCORSOrigin(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedOrigin string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualOrigin := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Origin\")\n\tif actualOrigin != expectedOrigin {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS origin '%s', got '%s'\", expectedOrigin, actualOrigin)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSMethods validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Methods header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS methods.\n// Common values include: \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\"\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSMethods(t, w, \"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\")\nfunc ValidateCORSMethods(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedMethods string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualMethods := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Methods\")\n\tif actualMethods != expectedMethods {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS methods '%s', got '%s'\", expectedMethods, actualMethods)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateCORSAllowHeaders validates the CORS Access-Control-Allow-Headers header value.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response has the expected CORS headers.\n// Common values include: \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\"\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t, w, \"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\")\nfunc ValidateCORSAllowHeaders(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedHeaders string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tactualHeaders := w.Header().Get(\"Access-Control-Allow-Headers\")\n\tif actualHeaders != expectedHeaders {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected CORS allow-headers '%s', got '%s'\", expectedHeaders, actualHeaders)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageContains validates that the error message contains expected text.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message contains the specified substring.\n// This is useful for verifying that error messages are descriptive and helpful.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorMessageContains(t, w, \"not found\")\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageContains(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, expectedText string) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, expectedText) {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected error message to contain '%s', got '%s'\", expectedText, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorMessageMinLength validates that the error message meets minimum length.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response message is at least the specified\n// number of characters. This ensures error messages are sufficiently descriptive.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t, w, 15)\nfunc ValidateErrorMessageMinLength(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder, minLength int) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\ts3Err := ParseErrorResponse(t, w.Body.Bytes())\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < minLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), minLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateXMLDeclaration validates that the response starts with an XML declaration.\n//\n// This helper function checks that the error response begins with \"<?xml\" which indicates\n// a proper XML declaration. S3 error responses should be valid XML documents.\n//\n// Example:\n//   ValidateXMLDeclaration(t, w)\nfunc ValidateXMLDeclaration(t *testing.T, w *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tbody := w.Body.String()\n\n\tif !strings.HasPrefix(body, \"<?xml\") {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected response to start with XML declaration, got: %s\", body[:min(len(body), 50)])\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR RESPONSE STRUCTURE VALIDATION\n// =============================================================================\n// These helpers provide comprehensive error response structure validation\n// with support for optional field validation and detailed error reporting.\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorStructureValidationOptions configures error structure validation.\ntype ErrorStructureValidationOptions struct {\n\t// RequireCode ensures the error code field is present and non-empty\n\tRequireCode bool\n\t// RequireMessage ensures the error message field is present and non-empty\n\tRequireMessage bool\n\t// ExpectedCode checks that the error code matches the expected value\n\tExpectedCode string\n\t// MinMessageLength ensures the error message meets minimum length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\t// MessageContains checks that the error message contains specific text\n\tMessageContains string\n\t// CustomFields validates additional custom fields in the error response\n\tCustomFields map[string]string\n}\n\n// DefaultValidationOptions returns default validation options.\nfunc DefaultValidationOptions() ErrorStructureValidationOptions {\n\treturn ErrorStructureValidationOptions{\n\t\tRequireCode:      true,\n\t\tRequireMessage:   true,\n\t\tMinMessageLength: 10,\n\t\tCustomFields:     make(map[string]string),\n\t}\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructure validates the structure of an error response.\n//\n// This helper function performs comprehensive validation of error response structure:\n// - Validates response body is not empty\n// - Validates error field exists (Code field)\n// - Validates error message is present and non-empty\n// - Supports optional field validation via options\n//\n// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n// Note: This function does not call t.Error() to allow testing of invalid cases.\n// Use AssertValidErrorResponseStructure() for assertion behavior.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   t: Testing instance for context\n//   body: Response body as byte array\n//   options: Validation options (use DefaultValidationOptions() for defaults)\n//\n// Example:\n//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n//   opts.RequireCode = true\n//   opts.RequireMessage = true\n//   opts.MinMessageLength = 15\n//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody, opts)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Check response body is not empty\n\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse the error response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Track validation results\n\tallValid := true\n\n\t// Validate error code field exists if required\n\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\tallValid = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate error message field exists if required\n\tif options.RequireMessage {\n\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tif trimmedMsg == \"\" {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate expected code if specified\n\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tallValid = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate minimum message length if specified\n\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 {\n\t\t// Use trimmed message for length validation\n\t\ttrimmedMsg := strings.TrimSpace(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tif len(trimmedMsg) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate message contains expected text if specified (case-insensitive)\n\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" {\n\t\tmessageLower := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tkeywordLower := strings.ToLower(options.MessageContains)\n\t\tif !strings.Contains(messageLower, keywordLower) {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate custom fields if specified\n\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n\t\t\tallValid = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn allValid\n}\n\n// ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple validates error response structure with defaults.\n//\n// This is a simplified version that uses default validation options.\n// It validates:\n// - Response body is not empty\n// - Error code field exists and is non-empty\n// - Error message field exists and is non-empty (min 10 chars)\n//\n// Returns true if validation passes, false otherwise.\n//\n// Example:\n//   valid := ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, responseBody)\nfunc ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t *testing.T, body []byte) bool {\n\tt.Helper()\n\treturn ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, DefaultValidationOptions())\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructure validates error structure and asserts if invalid.\n//\n// This helper validates error response structure and fails the test if validation fails.\n// Use this when you want test execution to stop on validation failure.\n// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n//\n// Example:\n//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t, responseBody)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructure(t *testing.T, body []byte) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructureSimple(t, body) {\n\t\t// Provide detailed error message\n\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif len(s3Err.Message) < 10 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least 10): %s\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions validates with custom options and asserts.\n//\n// This helper validates error response structure with custom options and fails the test\n// if validation fails. Use this when you need specific validation requirements.\n// Provides detailed error messages for debugging.\n//\n// Example:\n//   opts := DefaultValidationOptions()\n//   opts.MinMessageLength = 20\n//   opts.MessageContains = \"authentication\"\n//   AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t, responseBody, opts)\nfunc AssertValidErrorResponseStructureWithOptions(t *testing.T, body []byte, options ErrorStructureValidationOptions) {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\tif !ValidateErrorResponseStructure(t, body, options) {\n\t\t// Provide detailed error message based on options\n\t\tif len(body) == 0 {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: response body is empty\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &s3Err); err != nil {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: invalid XML: %v\", err)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.RequireCode && s3Err.Code == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Code field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.RequireMessage && s3Err.Message == \"\" {\n\t\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed: missing Message field\")\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.ExpectedCode != \"\" && s3Err.Code != options.ExpectedCode {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected code '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\toptions.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.MinMessageLength > 0 && len(s3Err.Message) < options.MinMessageLength {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: message too short (%d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\t\tlen(s3Err.Message), options.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif options.MessageContains != \"\" && !strings.Contains(s3Err.Message, options.MessageContains) {\n\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected message to contain '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\toptions.MessageContains, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tfor field, expectedValue := range options.CustomFields {\n\t\t\tactualValue := getXMLField(body, field)\n\t\t\tif actualValue != expectedValue {\n\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Error response structure validation failed: expected field '%s' to be '%s', got '%s'\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfield, expectedValue, actualValue)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tt.Fatal(\"Error response structure validation failed\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// getXMLField extracts a field value from XML response body.\n// This is a helper for validating custom fields in error responses.\nfunc getXMLField(body []byte, field string) string {\n\t// Simple XML field extraction using string search\n\t// This is basic but works for common S3 error response structures\n\topenTag := \"<\" + field + \">\"\n\tcloseTag := \"</\" + field + \">\"\n\n\tbodyStr := string(body)\n\topenIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr, openTag)\n\tif openIdx == -1 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tcloseIdx := strings.Index(bodyStr[openIdx:], closeTag)\n\tif closeIdx == -1 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.TrimSpace(bodyStr[openIdx+len(openTag):openIdx+closeIdx])\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// REQUEST CREATION HELPERS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// CreateTestRequest creates an HTTP request for testing.\n//\n// This helper centralizes request creation and ensures consistent test setup.\n// It supports:\n// - Custom HTTP methods and paths\n// - Optional request body\n// - Custom headers\n// - Test server URL format\nfunc CreateTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string, body io.Reader, headers map[string]string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, body)\n\n\t// Set default headers for test requests\n\tif headers == nil {\n\t\theaders = make(map[string]string)\n\t}\n\n\t// Set custom headers\n\tfor key, value := range headers {\n\t\treq.Header.Set(key, value)\n\t}\n\n\treturn req\n}\n\n// CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest creates a properly authenticated test request.\n//\n// This helper creates a request with valid AWS4-HMAC-SHA256 authentication.\n// It's useful for testing non-authentication errors (where auth should succeed).\nfunc CreateAuthenticatedTestRequest(t *testing.T, method, path string) *http.Request {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\t// Use the working authentication helper from auth_integration_test.go\n\treturn createSignedRequestForAuthTest(t, method, path, \"\", \"TESTACCESSKEY\", \"TESTSECRETKEY123456789012345678901234\", nil)\n}\n\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TEST SCENARIO STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorScenario represents a testable error scenario.\ntype ErrorScenario struct {\n\t// Name is the scenario name for test reporting\n\tName string\n\n\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n\n\t// ExpectedCode is the expected S3 error code\n\tExpectedCode string\n\n\t// ExpectedStatus is the expected HTTP status code\n\tExpectedStatus int\n\n\t// ExpectedKeywords are keywords that should appear in the error message\n\tExpectedKeywords []string\n\n\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length\n\tMinMessageLength int\n\n\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n\n\t// Description explains what this scenario tests\n\tDescription string\n}\n\n// ErrorScenarioResult captures the result of running a scenario.\ntype ErrorScenarioResult struct {\n\tScenarioName string\n\tStatusCode   int\n\tErrorCode    string\n\tMessage      string\n\tDuration     time.Duration\n\tPassed       bool\n}\n\n// RunErrorScenario executes a single error scenario and returns the result.\nfunc RunErrorScenario(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorScenarioResult {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treq := scenario.SetupRequest(t)\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\n\t// Measure response time\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\tfixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\n\t// Parse response\n\tvar s3Err S3Error\n\txml.Unmarshal(w.Body.Bytes(), &s3Err)\n\n\t// Build result\n\tresult := &ErrorScenarioResult{\n\t\tScenarioName: scenario.Name,\n\t\tStatusCode:   w.Code,\n\t\tErrorCode:    s3Err.Code,\n\t\tMessage:      s3Err.Message,\n\t\tDuration:     duration,\n\t}\n\n\t// Validate result\n\tresult.Passed = true\n\n\tif w.Code != scenario.ExpectedStatus {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected status %d, got %d\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedStatus, w.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif s3Err.Code != scenario.ExpectedCode {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Expected error code '%s', got '%s'\", scenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedCode, s3Err.Code)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\tif len(s3Err.Message) < scenario.MinMessageLength {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message too short (got %d chars, want at least %d): %s\",\n\t\t\tscenario.Name, len(s3Err.Message), scenario.MinMessageLength, s3Err.Message)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\t// Check keywords if specified\n\tif len(scenario.ExpectedKeywords) > 0 {\n\t\tmessage := strings.ToLower(s3Err.Message)\n\t\tfound := false\n\t\tfor _, keyword := range scenario.ExpectedKeywords {\n\t\t\tif strings.Contains(message, strings.ToLower(keyword)) {\n\t\t\t\tfound = true\n\t\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t\tif !found {\n\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Error message should contain at least one of %v, got: %s\",\n\t\t\t\tscenario.Name, scenario.ExpectedKeywords, s3Err.Message)\n\t\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Check response time if specified\n\tif scenario.MaxResponseTime > 0 && duration > scenario.MaxResponseTime {\n\t\tt.Errorf(\"%s: Response time %v exceeds maximum %v\", scenario.Name, duration, scenario.MaxResponseTime)\n\t\tresult.Passed = false\n\t}\n\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASE TEST STRUCTURES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorTestSuite provides a base structure for error response test suites.\n//\n// This structure helps organize related error tests and provides\n// consistent reporting and validation across test types.\ntype ErrorTestSuite struct {\n\tt          *testing.T\n\tname       string\n\tfixture    *TestServerFixture\n\tscenarios  []ErrorScenario\n\tresults    []*ErrorScenarioResult\n}\n\n// NewErrorTestSuite creates a new error test suite.\nfunc NewErrorTestSuite(t *testing.T, name string) *ErrorTestSuite {\n\tt.Helper()\n\n\treturn &ErrorTestSuite{\n\t\tt:       t,\n\t\tname:    name,\n\t\tfixture: NewTestServer(t),\n\t\tresults: make([]*ErrorScenarioResult, 0),\n\t}\n}\n\n// AddScenario adds a test scenario to the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) AddScenario(scenario ErrorScenario) *ErrorTestSuite {\n\tsuite.scenarios = append(suite.scenarios, scenario)\n\treturn suite\n}\n\n// Run executes all scenarios in the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) Run() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// RunSequentially executes all scenarios sequentially (not in parallel).\n// Use this when scenarios have dependencies or share state.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) RunSequentially() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tsuite.t.Run(suite.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\tfor _, scenario := range suite.scenarios {\n\t\t\tt.Run(scenario.Name, func(t *testing.T) {\n\t\t\t\tt.Parallel() // Disable parallelism\n\t\t\t\tresult := RunErrorScenario(t, suite.fixture, scenario)\n\t\t\t\tsuite.results = append(suite.results, result)\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t})\n}\n\n// ReportStatistics logs performance statistics for the suite.\nfunc (suite *ErrorTestSuite) ReportStatistics() {\n\tsuite.t.Helper()\n\n\tif len(suite.results) == 0 {\n\t\tsuite.t.Log(\"No results to report\")\n\t\treturn\n\t}\n\n\tvar total, max, min time.Duration\n\tmin = 1 * time.Second // Initialize to high value\n\tpassed := 0\n\tfailed := 0\n\n\tfor _, result := range suite.results {\n\t\ttotal += result.Duration\n\t\tif result.Duration > max {\n\t\t\tmax = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Duration < min {\n\t\t\tmin = result.Duration\n\t\t}\n\t\tif result.Passed {\n\t\t\tpassed++\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfailed++\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tavg := total / time.Duration(len(suite.results))\n\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"Error Test Suite: %s\", suite.name)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Total scenarios: %d\", len(suite.results))\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Passed: %d, Failed: %d\", passed, failed)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Average response time: %v\", avg)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Min response time: %v\", min)\n\tsuite.t.Logf(\"  Max response time: %v\", max)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// UTILITY FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// min returns the minimum of two integers.\nfunc min(a, b int) int {\n\tif a < b {\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\treturn b\n}\n\n// MeasureRequestTime measures the time to execute a request.\n// Returns the duration and response recorder.\nfunc MeasureRequestTime(handler http.Handler, req *http.Request) (time.Duration, *httptest.ResponseRecorder) {\n\tw := httptest.NewRecorder()\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\thandler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\tduration := time.Since(start)\n\treturn duration, w\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DOCUMENTATION AND EXAMPLES\n// =============================================================================\n\n/*\nExample Usage:\n\nBasic error response validation:\n\n    func TestMyError(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        req := CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/nonexistent\", nil, nil)\n        w := httptest.NewRecorder()\n        fixture.Handler.ServeHTTP(w, req)\n\n        VerifyStandardErrorResponse(t, w, \"NoSuchKey\", 404)\n    }\n\nAdvanced validation with fluent API:\n\n    func TestMyErrorAdvanced(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n CORSOrigin(\"*\").\n            CORSMethods(\"GET, PUT, DELETE, HEAD, POST, OPTIONS\").\n            CORSHeaders(\"Authorization, Content-Type, Range, Content-Length\").\n            Assert()\n \"Scenario 1\",\n                SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                    return CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/key1\", nil, nil)\n                },\n        suite.Run()\n        suite.ReportStatistics()\n    }\n\nUsing error scenarios directly:\n\n    func TestMyScenario(t *testing.T) {\n        fixture := NewTestServer(t)\n\n        scenario := ErrorScenario{\n            Name: \"My custom scenario\",\n            SetupRequest: func(t *testing.T) *http.Request {\n                return CreateTestRequest(t, \"GET\", \"/test-bucket/test\", required\",\n518\t\t\t\"\",\n519\t\t)\n520\t)\n521\t\n522\t// =============================================================================\n523\t// FIXTURE RETRIEVAL FUNCTIONS\n524\t// =============================================================================\n525\t\n526\t// GetFixtureByStatusCode returns a fixture for the given HTTP status code.\n527\t//\n528\t// Args:\n529\t//   statusCode: HTTP status code\n530\t//   path: Optional resource path\n531\t//\n532\t// Returns:\n533\t//   *HTTPErrorFixture for the given status code\n534\t//\n535\t// Raises:\n536\t//   panic if status code is not supported\n537\t//\n538\t// Example:\n539\t//   fixture := GetFixtureByStatusCode(404, \"/api/file.txt\")\n540\t//   fixture := GetFixtureByStatusCode(405, \"/api/file.txt\")\n541\tfunc GetFixtureByStatusCode(statusCode int, path string) *HTTPErrorFixture {\n542\t\tswitch statusCode {\n543\t\tcase 400:\n544\t\t\treturn BadRequestFixture(path, \"\", \"\")\n545\t\tcase 401:\n546\t\t\treturn UnauthorizedFixture(path, \"\", \"\")\n547\t\tcase 403:\n548\t\t\treturn ForbiddenFixture(path, \"\", \"\")\n549\t\tcase 404:\n550\t\t\treturn NotFoundFixture(path, \"\", \"\")\n551\t\tcase 405:\n552\t\t\treturn MethodNotAllowedFixture(path, \"GET, HEAD\", \"\", \"\")\n553\t\tcase 415:\n554\t\t\treturn UnsupportedMediaTypeFixture(path, It extends the infrastructure in error_test_infrastructure.go\n15\t// with organized patterns for common error scenarios.\n16\t//\n17\t// Design Philosophy:\n18\t// - Table-driven tests for consistency\n19\t// - Composable patterns for flexibility\n20\t// - Clear extension points for custom scenarios\n21\t// - Self-documenting test structures\n22\t//\n23\t// Usage:\n24\t//   1. Choose a base pattern that matches your error type\n25\t//   2. Extend the test table with your specific scenarios\n26\t//   3. Use the provided helpers to execute tests\n27\t//   4. Leverage predefined validators for common assertions\n28\t// =============================================================================\n29\t\n30\t// =============================================================================\n31\t// BASE TEST TABLE STRUCTURES\n32\t// =============================================================================\n33\t\n34\t// CommonErrorTestCase is the base structure for all error test cases.\n35\t// This structure captures the common elements across all error types.\n36\ttype CommonErrorTestCase struct {\n37\t\t// Name is the test case name for reporting\n38\t\tName string\n39\t\n40\t\t// Description explains what this test case validates\n41\t\tDescription string\n42\t\n43\t\t// SetupRequest creates the request that triggers the error\n44\t\tSetupRequest func(*testing.T) *http.Request\n45\t\n46\t\t// ExpectedStatus is []string\n57\t\n58\t\t// MinMessageLength is the minimum acceptable message length (default: 15)\n59\t\tMinMessageLength int\n60\t\n61\t\t// ValidateResponse provides custom validation beyond the standard checks\n62\t\tValidateResponse func(*testing.T, *httptest.ResponseRecorder)\n63\t\n64\t\t// MaxResponseTime is the maximum acceptable response duration\n65\t\tMaxResponseTime time.Duration\n66\t\n67\t\t// SkipCORSValidation disables CORS header checks (for non-CORS scenarios)\n68\t\tSkipCORSValidation bool\n69\t}\n70\t\n71\t// AuthenticationErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for auth-specific errors.\n72\ttype AuthenticationErrorTestCase struct {\n73\t\tCommonErrorTestCase\n74\t\n75\t\t// AccessKey used in the request (for from auth verification\n82\t\tExpectedAuthError error\n83\t}\n84\t\n85\t// NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase extends CommonErrorTestCase for non-auth errors.\n86\ttype NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase =============================================================================\n434\t\n435\t// RunAuthenticationTestCase executes a single authentication error test case.\n436\t// It performs standard validation plus authentication-specific checks.\n437\tfunc RunAuthenticationTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt AuthenticationErrorTestCase) {\n438\t\tt.Helper()\n439\t\n440\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n441\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n442\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n443\t\n444\t\t// Standard validation\n445\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n446\t\n447\t\t// Authentication-specific validation\n448\t\tif tt.ExpectedAuthError != nil {\n449\t\t\t// The error response should indicate the auth failure type\n450\t\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n451\t\t\t\tMessageContainsAny(tt.AuthErrorType).\n452\t\t\t\tAssert()\n453\t\t}\n454\t}\n455\t\n456\t// RunNonAuthenticationTestCase executes a single non-authentication error test case.\n457\t// It performs standard validation for non-auth errors.\n458\tfunc RunNonAuthenticationTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt NonAuthenticationErrorTestCase) {\n459\t\tt.Helper()\n460\t\n461\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n462\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n463\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n464\t\n465\t\t// Standard validation\n466\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t// RunCORSTestCase executes a single CORS error test case.\n470\t// It performs standard validation plus CORS-specific checks.\n471\tfunc RunCORSTestCase(t *testing.T, fixture *TestServerFixture, tt CORSErrorTestCase) {\n472\t\tt.Helper()\n473\t\n474\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n475\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n476\t\tduration, w := MeasureRequestTime(fixture.Handler, req)\n477\t\n478\t\t// Standard validation\n479\t\tRunCommonErrorValidations(t, w, tt.CommonErrorTestCase, duration)\n480\t\n481\t\t// CORS-specific validation\n482\t\tVerifyErrorResponse(t, w).\n483\t\t\tCORSOrigin(tt.ExpectedCORSOrigin).\n484\t\t\tCORSMethods(tt.ExpectedCORSMethods).\n485\t\t\tCORSHeaders(tt.ExpectedCORSHeaders).\n486\t\t\tAssert()\n487\t}\n488\t\n489\t// fixture *TestServerFixture, tt ContentTypeErrorTestCase) {\n492\t\tt.Helper()\n493\t\n494\t\t// Create request and measure response time\n495\t\treq := tt.SetupRequest(t)\n496\t\tduration, w :=
